key_insight: Capillary thinning is not just a way to measure one relaxation time; it is a benchmark complex flow that reveals how constitutive models capture coupled geometry–microstructure dynamics in viscoelastic polymer solutions.
engineering_relevance: The Pipkin-diagram framework helps engineers compare polymer solutions and devices using universal thinning behavior, improving model selection for extensional-flow applications such as spraying, coating, and filament breakup.
method: Recast capillary-breakup rheometry output as a self-selected elastic strain rate via an elastic Weissenberg number Wi_e.
Organize results in an elastocapillary Pipkin diagram with Wi_e versus a geometry-controlled Deborah number.
Use a mid-filament stress balance and scaling analysis to collapse families of thinning curves onto a master curve, then test constitutive models such as C2D2 and FENE-P.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that the framework organizes data spanning decades in molecular weight and concentration across multiple devices, and that C2D2 matches the observed plateau behavior while classical FENE-P does not.
watch_item: The approach relies on careful interpretation of geometry-dependent onset and initial prestretch, so experimental protocol and device details can strongly affect the inferred benchmark behavior.
paper_title: To win, a model must thin: Capillary thinning as a benchmark complex flow for constitutive models of viscoelastic polymer solutions
